text:
There once was an old, wise squirrel named Rufus, who learned many fascinating things about life. One sunny morning, Rufus sat upon his favorite branch overlooking the garden where all his relatives lived.

He watched them interacting with each other. There was Sally, the adventurous one who always wanted to explore new things. There was Jerry, the philosopher, who was always contemplating. And finally, there was Momma, the keeper of the secret garden secrets.

One day, Sally approached Rufus excitedly.

"Squirrel, do you think bats talk?" she whispered.

Rufus considered this for a moment. "Oh, I think they do," he replied mysteriously. "You know—you hear their little 'chirps' from high up?"

That night, Sally prowled around the attic searching for the elusive bat voices she'd heard.

Meanwhile, Jerry was caught in thought again. "Has anyone ever really thought about what time we die?" he pondered aloud.
